title: podman-restart.service Does Not Restart "unless-stopped" Containers
body:
### Issue Description

The podman-restart.service daemon only restarts services that are in an "always" restart state. This is inconsistent with the behavior of Docker which will restart all running "unless-stopped" containers after a system-wide restart.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Use docker-compose (or similar tool) to start a few containers with "restart: unless-stopped" configuration.
2. Reboot the system with the podman-restart.service daemon enabled.

### Describe the results you received

All containers with the "unless-stopped" configuration are not restarted by podman-restart.service.

### Describe the results you expected

All running containers with a "unless-stopped" configuration get restarted by podman-restart.service upon system boot.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

RHEL 9.1 running on Proxmox 7.3-6

### Additional information

Containers were created using docker-compose 2.16.0. Docker's restart policy documentation is available at: https://docs.docker.com/config/containers/start-containers-automatically/

comments.body: Looks like this is fixed in the main branch. And will be delivered in podman 4.4 (RHEL9.2)
